content:
- Started Choose-Task flow.
- Read prior progress log, list-active-tasks skill, current active-task summary, and completed-task summary.
- Initial state shows one active failing task in story-08 and its blocker task-01 already passes true in current_tasks_done.
- Next step is to refresh Ralph task summaries, re-check blocker resolution, then write the chosen task path to .ralph/current_task.txt and quit.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries with .ralph/update_task_summary.sh.
- Re-read current_tasks, current_tasks_done, and ralph-choose-task rules.
- Verified there are no active failing bug tasks visible in the current summary.
- Verified the only active failing non-bug task is .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-03-upgrade-to-go-1-26-2.md.
- Verified its blocker .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-01-github-actions-multiarch-ghcr.md already has <passes>true</passes> in current_tasks_done.
- Confirmed actual repo stack from codebase shape: Go module with Makefile, cmd/pg_gobench, and internal packages.
- Next step is to write the selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt and quit immediately.

- Wrote .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Selected .ralph/tasks/story-08-release-docs/task-03-upgrade-to-go-1-26-2.md as the next task.
- Reason: it is the only active unblocked failing task after summary refresh; there are no active failing bug tasks, and its blocker task-01 already passes true.
- Quit immediately after this selection.

- Read previous progress entries and confirmed the selected task path from .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Loaded the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skills for this turn.
- Next step: inspect the selected task file and determine whether it is in planning or execution state.

- Read the selected task file and confirmed it is still in planning state.
- The task description explicitly exempts this work from TDD because it is toolchain/release plumbing, so the next action is to write a plan rather than implement.
- Next step: inspect existing Ralph plan conventions, then create the plan file and update the task to point at it with a verification marker.

- Inspected existing plan files and task-file plan-pointer convention.
- Confirmed this turn should create a new plan file and add a <plan> pointer into the selected task markdown.
- Next step: inspect the repository for Go version pins so the plan covers every owned toolchain boundary.

- Confirmed the task markdown format and the need for a <plan> pointer.
- Started scanning the repository for Go version pins, CI references, and release-path boundaries so the plan covers the real owned surfaces.
- Next step: write the plan file with explicit verification and boundary-cleanup slices, then update the task file to point at it.

- Verified the remaining explicit Go version pins: go.mod and Dockerfile.
- Confirmed CI currently derives its toolchain from go.mod via actions/setup-go go-version-file, which is the clean boundary to preserve.
- Next step: create the task-03 plan file, add the task <plan> pointer, promote the plan to NOW EXECUTE if the design stays clean, then quit immediately.

- Created the task-03 Go 1.26.2 upgrade plan under the task-local _plans directory.
- Added the <plan> pointer to the selected task markdown and promoted the plan to NOW EXECUTE because the version-ownership boundary is clear.
- Next step for the next turn: execute the plan, run make check/lint/test plus docker build, then push and verify the GHCR workflow and published SHA tag.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skills.
- Confirmed the selected task is story-08 task-03 and previous work already moved it to execution state.
- Next: read the task markdown and referenced plan, then execute only if the design still holds.

- Read the selected task markdown and confirmed it points to an execution plan.
- Confirmed the task description exempts this plumbing/release work from behavior-style TDD while still requiring full lint/test verification.
- Next: read the approved plan and inspect every repo-owned Go version boundary before editing files.

- Read the execution plan and re-scanned the repo for owned Go version pins.
- Confirmed only go.mod and Dockerfile currently pin 1.25.5, which matches the planned boundary.
- Next: inspect those files plus the GHCR workflow boundary, then patch the version pins and start validation.

- Patched go.mod and Dockerfile from Go 1.25.5 to Go 1.26.2.
- Preserved the existing clean CI boundary where actions/setup-go reads go-version-file from go.mod.
- Next: run stale-reference scan, make check, make lint, make test, and a real Docker build.

- Local validation passed: make check, make lint, make test, and docker build all succeeded after the Go 1.26.2 upgrade.
- No stale 1.25.5 repo-owned references remain, and the workflow boundary still derives Go from go.mod.
- Next: prepare the Git/GitHub verification path, push an upgrade commit, and verify the GHCR workflow plus SHA-tagged image remotely.

- Created and pushed the upgrade commit so there is an exact remote SHA to verify.
- Commit pushed to origin/master after local check/lint/test and Docker validation passed.
- Next: poll the GitHub Actions run for this head SHA and verify the SHA-tagged GHCR image exists.

- The pushed upgrade commit triggered Publish GHCR Image run #18 for head SHA 43c18e3ec9d986e55525d1457412699627ea94f6.
- GitHub Actions validate/build/publish completed green for that SHA after both amd64 and arm64 build legs succeeded.
- Next: verify the exact SHA tag in GHCR, then mark the task done, task-switch, and create the required final task-finished commit.
